items.name: Almond Concentrate

sections.html:
• Model
  • Icon
  • Former Icon
sections.title: Almond Concentrate

sections.html:
File Name

N/A

Icon Name

N/A

Almond Concentrate is an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a bottle.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: Almond Concentrate is a white bottle with a blue cap used to seal it. According to the label, the bottle contains "Raw Almond Concentrate".
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • Abandoned Outpost
sections.title: Location

sections.html:
Using the Item

Almond Concentrate cannot be used.

Purpose

In Abandoned Outpost, 4 Bottles of Almond Concentrate can be found. Once you have all 4, put them into the Filtering Unit, and the Emergency Shower will be able to be used to cure the Wretch in the Outpost.
sections.title: Use

sections.html:
On the Bottle, there is a label with words. The label reads:

NOT FOR HUMAN CONSUMPTION

This product contains raw almond concentrate which can be fatal if consumed in large quantities. Keep away from pets and small children.
sections.title: Label

sections.html: • Along with the Thermometer, Almond Concentrate is one of the only Items in the Game that cannot be used.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: Can be used to fill emergency showers

items.name: Almond Water

sections.html:
• Model
  • Icon
  • Halloween (2023-2024)
  • Former Model

sections.title: Almond Water

sections.html:
File Name

Almond_Water_Can_with_texture

Icon Name

T_UI_Inv_Icon_Can

Almond Water is an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a can with a drink in it.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: Almond Water is a liquid stored in a yellow can. The can has the name of the item and a fancy design. At the top there's a gray metal opening, and it's used to restore Sanity.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • Level 1 - Habitable Zone
  • Overgrowth
  • Level 3 - Electrical Station
  • Level 4 - Abandoned Office
  • Level 5 - Terror Hotel
  • Level Fun
  • The End
  • Level 94
  • Level 6 - Lights Out
  • Level 7 - Thalassophobia
  • Level 8 - Cave System
  • The Manilla Room
  • Level 0.11
  • Level 9 - Darkened Suburbs
  • Level 10
  • Level 0.2
  • The Snackrooms
  • Level 188
  • Level Fun+
  • Level 52
  • Level 55.1
sections.title: Location

sections.html: Once used, the player opens the can and drinks the Almond Water, then throwing it off to the right. After drinking it, your Sanity will be restored by 50%.
sections.title: Use

sections.html:
• Almond Water was apparently crafted in The Poolrooms, which is mentioned on the can.
  • Almond Water is the only Item in the game with a Common rarity.
  • Overgrowth is the only secret level where Almond Water can be found, likely due to the fact that it's the only secret level that drains Sanity.
  • Almond Water can be found in cans that are about 13.5 cm high and about 5.2 cm wide. For comparison: a 250ml Redbull Energy can has exactly (maybe a few millimeters off) these measurements, whereas the Almond Water cans look similar in size and width to exactly this kind of can.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: Restores your sanity levels

items.name: Bug Spray

sections.html:

  • Former Icon
sections.title: Bug Spray

sections.html:
File Name

BP_Item_BugSpray

Icon Name

T_UI_Inv_Icon_BugSpray

Bug Spray is an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a spray can.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: Bug Spray is a silver spray can with a green label placed over it. On the label is a yellow banner with a bug on it. The bug has a red mark over it to indicate that it can kill bugs.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • Level 5
  • Level 9
sections.title: Location

sections.html:
Using the Item

Upon using, the substance inside the can will be sprayed out for a couple seconds. Bug Spray can be used as many times as you'd like.

Purpose

If the substance comes into contact with Male Deathmoths, they will turn into Moth Jelly.
sections.title: Use

sections.html: • While Male Deathmoths are small enough to be killed with a simple Bug Spray can, Female Deathmoths cannot, and require a lot more Bug Spray just to be fended off.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: Kills swarms of bugs

items.name: Camera

sections.html:
• Model
  • Icon
  • Former Icon
sections.title: Camera

sections.html:
File Name

N/A

Icon Name

N/A

Cameras are an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a classic camera.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: Cameras resemble a classic vintage camera from the 2010's. It is made of metal and plastic, and it comes with a built-in light. The Camera's light is not as strong as a Flashlight, but it can still be useful if you need to see an area without switching the Item you are holding. The Camera also has an image attached to the back side.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • Level 55.1
sections.title: Location

sections.html:
Using the Item

Upon using, the player will snap a photo of what's Infront of them.

Purpose

The main purpose of Cameras is to take a photo of what is shown in the picture on the back side. After capturing what's shown, a different photo will appear, and the player has to capture that new image. The player needs to take 6 photos of different objects. It can also be used to kill Smilers by facing them since the Camera has a light.
sections.title: Use

sections.html: • If you're in other Levels than Level 55.1, the Camera glitches and the player can see the first photo that needs to be taken.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: Used to take photos and upload them to the M.E.G. network

Chainsaws are an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to simply be a Chainsaw.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: Chainsaws looks like a typical Chainsaw with an orange handle. It cannot be used as a Weapon, despite being a Chainsaw, but it can be used to break boards.

This Item can be found in:

  • The Manilla Room
  • Level 0.11
  • Level !~! - Hotel Chase
sections.title: Location

Using the Item

Upon using, the Chainsaw will start moving for a short amount of time.

Purpose

If the Chainsaw comes into contact with certain wood if near it when in use, the wood will break. Some wood needs multiple uses of the Chainsaw to break.
sections.title: Use

sections.html: • Farmer Facelings can be seen holding Chainsaws.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: Can be used to tear down wooden barriers

This Item can be found in:

  • Level 4 - Abandoned Office
  • Abandoned Outpost
sections.title: Location

Using the Item

Upon using, the Crowbar is swung by the player.

Purpose

If the Crowbar is nearby any breakable Vent, the Vent will break open, allowing players to crouch and enter it.

Diving Helmets are an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a standard Diving Helmet.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: Diving Helmets are simply standard Diving Helmets that can be used for aquatic explorations.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • Level 7 - Thalassophobia
sections.title: Location

sections.html:
Using the Item

Upon using, the player will put the Diving Helmet onto their head. Once it's on, that player will be unable to view your Inventory and use Items.

Purpose

When wearing the Diving Helmet, you will be able to swim underwater. The only water that is only swimmable with the Diving Helmet though is the ocean of Level 7.
sections.title: Use

items.name: Energy Bar

sections.html:
• Model
  • Icon
  • Former Model
  • Former Icon
sections.title: Energy Bar

sections.html:
File Name

BP_EnergyBar

Icon Name

T_UI_Inv_Icon_EnergyBar

Energy Bars are an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a chocolate bar.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: Energy Bars are chocolate bars that give one energy. The wrapper is half red and half brown and says, "Dark Chocolate Cacao", seemingly being the flavor of the bar. It also says that there is a 35g source of protein in the bar. On the inside is a chocolate bar with little bits of chocolate.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • Overgrowth
  • Level 3 - Electrical Station
  • Level 4 - Abandoned Office
  • Level 5 - Terror Hotel
  • Level Fun
  • The End
  • Level 6 - Lights Out
  • Level 7 - Thalassophobia
  • Level 8 - Cave System
  • The Manilla Room
  • Level 0.11
  • Level 9 - Darkened Suburbs
  • Level 10
  • Level 0.2
  • The Snackrooms
  • Level 188
  • Level Fun+
  • Level 52
  • Level 55.1
sections.title: Location

sections.html: Upon using, the player opens up the Energy Bar and eats it. After consumption, your Sanity is restored by 25%. You'll also receive a Stamina Boost.
sections.title: Use

sections.html:
The Stamina boost allows the player to run for a total of 12.5 seconds and only goes away as the player uses their Stamina through running. Extra Stamina does not regenerate back (Extra stamina = stamina over the default stamina threshold. For example, in The Hub your default stamina threshold is 4 seconds, meaning you can sprint for a total of 4 seconds before running out of breath. Once you eat an energy bar in The Hub, your stamina will increase to 12.5 seconds, however, it will not regenerate until it is below 4 seconds. For information on the stamina threshold of every level, see Base Stamina) Energy Bars do not stack, which means the maximum number of stamina you can have at a time is 12.5 seconds. In addition, an Energy Bar replenishes 25% Sanity.

The Player's Base Stamina is slightly different depending on the Level they are on. The recovery speed after completely running out of Stamina is always 7 seconds, but the regeneration speed is sometimes different, allowing you to run more if you wait for your Stamina to regenerate without allowing it to completely deplete.

  • Infinite: Level !~!
  • ~7.5 seconds: Level 9223372036854775807, Level Fun+ (sectors)
  • ~5.5 seconds: Level 3
  • ~4.5 seconds: Level 0, Level 2, Level 5, Level Fun, Level 37 Level ! , The End, Level 94, Level 6, Level 7, Level 8, Level 0.11 Level 9, Abandoned Outpost, Level 10, Level 3999, Level 0.1, The Snackrooms, Level 188, Level 37.2 , Level Fun+, Level 52, Level 55.1
  • ~4 seconds: The Hub, Level 4
  • ~3.5 seconds: Level 1

This only applies to the main areas in the listed Levels. Some Levels like Level Fun+ have other areas that have different values. Level Fun+ in this case has your base Stamina last for ~7.5 seconds when you're in one of the Sectors. More cases like this might be present but not tested.
sections.title: Stamina Boost

sections.html: Whole Milk Powder, Maltose Syrup, Coating Sugar, Vegetable Fat (Fully Hydrogenated Palm Kernel), Whey Powder, Skim Milk Powder, Cocoa Powder, Stabilizer, Lecithin, Flavoring, Sucrose, Egg Albumen, (Egg Albumen, Food Acid), Flavorings, Glycerol, Food Acid, Colorant, Vitamins, Calcium Carbonate, and Magnesium Oxide.
sections.title: Ingredients

sections.html:
• Overgrowth is the only secret level where Energy Bars can be found, likely due to the fact that it's the only secret level that drains Sanity.
  • Energy Bars grant stamina upon consumption, which might be a bit hard to tell since there's no stamina bar or indication (excluding the item description and the name energy bar) that it gives you a stamina boost or the stamina boost was given.
  • The flavor of the Energy Bar "Dark Chocolate Cacao" mentions cacao (also known as a cocoa bean or cocoa) in the name, which is the thing that chocolate comes from.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: Gives you a temporary stamina boost

items.name: Firework

sections.html:
• Model
  • Icon
  • Former Icon
sections.title: Firework

sections.html:
File Name

N/A

Icon Name

N/A

Fireworks are an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a red Firework.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: Fireworks are normal Fireworks that are wrapped with a shiny red clingfilm that has white stars.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • Level Fun+
sections.title: Location

sections.html:
Using the Item

Upon using, the player lights the Firework and throw it forward. After a couple seconds, the Firework explodes.

Purpose

Death to Players

If a player is too close to a Firework upon blowing up, it will kill the player.

Death to Party Hosts

If a Firework touches a Party Host, it will kill the Party Host, allowing progression of the Funhouse Sector in Level Fun+ and for the Party Host that was killed to permanently close its eye.
sections.title: Use

sections.html: • Along with Bug Spray, Fireworks are one of the only Items in the game that can kill certain entities.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: Can be used to stun Party Hosts

sections.html:

N/A

Icon Name

N/A


Flare Guns are an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a orange gun.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: Flare Guns are orange guns that shoots flares to clear out fog.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • Level 7 - Thalassophobia
sections.title: Location

sections.html:
Using the Item

Upon using, a Flare is shot out of the gun.

Purpose

The purpose of Flare Guns is for 2 or more players so you can let your teammates know where you're at using one, since the Flare shot out of the gun can be seen in the fog.
sections.title: Use

sections.html: • When The Flare Gun was first shown in Fancy's 6th Devlog, it had a Rarity of Epic, however this was changed for unknown reasons.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: Fires a flare to let your teammates let you know where you are

sections.title: Files

sections.html: Flashlights are 1990's to 2010-sized flashlights. It can be used to see in Dark Areas and to stun Hounds or make Smilers disappear.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • Level 0
  • Level 1 - Habitable Zone
  • The Bunker
  • Overgrowth
  • Level 2 - Pipe Dreams
  • Level 3 Electrical Station

Using the Item

Upon using, the player turns on the Flashlight and a Light is shined through the Flashlight.

The main purpose of Flashlights is to allow players to see in dark areas using the light, but a Flashlight can also be used to stun Hounds and keep them in place, and to make Smilers in Level 0.2 and Level 55.1 disappear.

sections.title: Glowstick

sections.html:
File Name

N/A

Icon Name

N/A

Glowsticks are an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a tube.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: Glowsticks are plastic tubes that glow different colors. They can be found in 4 different colors: Green, Red, Blue, and Yellow.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • Level Fun+
sections.title: Location

sections.html:
Using the Item

Upon using, the player drops the Glowstick on the ground.

Purpose

In the Arcade Sector of Level Fun+, different symbols can be found in the dark zone. If you are holding a certain Glowstick in front of each one, the respective symbol will light up. If you place down all of the correct colors in front of all 4 of the symbols, the locked door in the Sector will open.
sections.title: Use

sections.html:
• Glowsticks are the only Item in the game with variations.
  • Glowsticks can be found at the ditch in the final area of Level 8 with the color Orange, however they cannot be picked up.
  • If you drop all 4 Glowstick Colors in the same spot, they will glow up as white.
  • Glowsticks were first seen in Part 2, when they were found underneath the Tables in the locked door room at the end of Level Fun.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: Lights up areas and can be dropped to remember your path

items.name: Juice

sections.html:
• Model
  • Icon
  • Former Model
  • Former Icon
sections.title: Juice

sections.html:
File Name

BP_Juice

Icon Name

T_UI_Inv_Icon_Juice_v2png

Juice is an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a Juice in a bottle.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: Juice is a green liquid stored in a plastic bottle with a blue bottle cap.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • Overgrowth
  • Level 4 - Abandoned Office
  • Level 5 - Terror Hotel
  • Level Fun
  • Level 6 - Lights Out
  • Level 7 - Thalassophobia
  • Level 8 - Cave System
  • Level 0.11
  • Level 9 - Darkened Suburbs
  • Level 10
  • Level 0.2
  • The Snackrooms
  • Level 188
  • Level Fun+
  • Level 52
  • Level 55.1
sections.title: Location

sections.html: Upon using, the player will take off the cap and drink the Juice. Then, you'll receive a Speed Boost for a temporary amount of time (10 seconds), and your Sanity will go up by 25%. During the time of the Speed Boost, your Stamina is not drained.
sections.title: Use

sections.html:
In some Levels, the Speed Boost is slightly different. Because Juice sets your Speed to a specific value (rather than increasing it by a percentage), the Speed Boost lasts longer or shorter depending on what Level or location you're in.

  • 50%: Level 52
  • 35%: Level 3, Level 37, Level 6, Level 37.2
  • 22.7%: Level 0, The Hub, Level 4, Level 5, Level 7, Level 8, Level 0.11, Level 9, Abandoned Outpost, Level 3999, Level 0.1, The Snackrooms, Level !~!, Level 188, Level 55.1
  • 12.5%: Level 1, Level 2, Level Fun, Level !, The End, Level 9223372036854775807, Level 94, Level 10, Level Fun+
sections.title: Speed Boost

sections.html:
• The liquid in the bottle moves around with the player's movements.
  • Overgrowth is the only secret level where Juice can be found, likely due to the fact that it's the only secret level that drains Sanity.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: Gives you a temporary speed boost

items.name: Knife

sections.html:
• Model
  • Icon
sections.title: Knife

sections.html:
File Name

N/A

Icon Name

N/A

Knifes are an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a knife.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: Knifes resemble a metal combat knife with a wooden handle. It can be used to cut down ropes and Vines tangling players.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • The Grassrooms
sections.title: Location

sections.html:
Using the Item

Upon using, the player swings the Knife.

Purpose

Cutting the Ropes

If a swung Knife interacts with 3 different ropes around The Grassrooms, the ropes will break, allowing progression of the level.

Vine-Free Knifes

If you are playing with 2 or more people and a player gets stuck in Vines, it will take a few seconds before they die which gives other players time to use a Knife on them which will cut down the Vines, allowing the trapped player to become free.

Teammate Death

If you're playing with 2 or more people, the Knife can kill other players if they are hit by it, which will cause them to fall over and remain lifeless on the ground.
sections.title: Use

sections.html:
• A player's hazmat suit after getting killed by a Knife will become a ragdoll after a short death animation where they fall down.
  • In Fancy's 11th Devlog, he showed a player killing a player stuck in Vines with the Knife. Despite the fact that you can kill other players with a Knife, this would've freed the player from the Vines in-game.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: Can be used to cut ropes and vines

items.name: LiDAR Scanner

sections.html:
• Model
  • Icon
sections.title: LiDAR Scanner

sections.html:
File Name

N/A

Icon Name

N/A

LiDAR Scanners are an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a scanner.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: LiDAR Scanners are a yellowish/orange scanner with a screen connected to it, which shows a radar tracker. On the side of the Scanner, there is a keypad and a fingerprint scanner underneath. The keypad and fingerprint scanner currently have no use.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • Level 6 - Lights Out
sections.title: Location

sections.html:
Using the Item

Upon using, a bunch of dots get placed on walls for a few seconds, which only works in the 2 mazes of Level 6.

Purpose

Multiple Dot Colors

The dots that come out of the Scanner can be a bunch of different colors, and the colors change whenever you stop holding the Scanner and hold it again.

Tracker

When you're holding the Scanner, you can see the screen that shows the radar tracker. Every couple seconds, light will go around the area on the screen, and the Wretch in Level 6 will be shown as a red dot if it is within the area. Other players can be seen as green dots.
sections.title: Use

sections.html:
• There is no limit to how many dots can be on the walls at once.
  • The LiDAR Scanner is the only Item to not have an updated icon after Version 1.0.
    • The reason it doesn't have an updated icon is unknown.
  • The LiDAR Scanner was added so Level 6 isn't just bumping into walls in a pitch-black level until you find the exit.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: Fires glow in the dark dots

items.name: Liquid Pain

sections.html:
• Model
  • Icon
  • Former Model
  • Former Icon
sections.title: Liquid Pain

sections.html:
File Name

BP_Liquid_Pain

Icon Name

T_UI_Inv_Icon_Juice

Liquid Pain is an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a red liquid in a Bottle.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: Liquid pain is a red liquid stored in a plastic bottle with a blue bottle cap. Liquid Pain is a deadly Item, so do not drink it under any circumstance.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • Level Fun
  • Level 94
  • Level 0.11
  • Level 9 - Darkened Suburbs
  • Level 52
sections.title: Location

sections.html: Upon using, the player drinks the Liquid Pain. After consumption, the player will stop, and your screen will slowly go to black until the player dies.
sections.title: Use

sections.html:
• Liquid Pain is the only Item in the game with a Legendary rarity that can be found in more than one Level.
  • The liquid in the bottle moves around with the player's movements.
  • Liquid Pain is almost completely pointless, as all it is useful for is for an automatic death.
  • Liquid Pain has some details about it on its Backrooms Wikidot page that aren't mentioned anywhere in-game:
    • It is the product of a chemical reaction, resulting in the hydrogen cyanide reacting with the potassium chloride and creating hydrochloric acid and potassium cyanide.
    • The products mentioned above are the causes of its fatal effect.
    • It's stated that Liquid Pain is a variation of Almond Water.
    • Liquid Pain's object number is 48, because on the Wikidot all objects are sorted with numbers, similar to Entities and Levels on the original Backrooms wikis.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: Causes you a lot of pain...

items.name: Moth Jelly

sections.html:
• Model
  • Icon
  • Former Icon
sections.title: Moth Jelly

sections.html:
File Name

Wax_bar

Icon Name

T_UI_Inv_Icon_WaxBar

Moth Jelly is an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be rectangular piece of purple jelly.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: Moth jelly is a shiny purple rectangle made of Jelly, and it closely resembles grape Jelly. It can only be obtained by using Bug Spray on Male Deathmoths.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • Level 5 - Terror Hotel
  • Level 9 - Darkened Suburbs
sections.title: Location

sections.html: Upon using, the player eats the Moth Jelly. After this, you receive a Speed Boost for a temporary amount of time. Your Sanity and Stamina will also be restored.
sections.title: Use

sections.html: Moth Jelly provides the player with the benefits of an Energy Bar and Juice combined, meaning an increase in Speed for 10 seconds, fully restoring your Stamina and granting an additional, temporary 12.5 seconds of Stamina that will not regenerate, but is only used while the player is Running (the Stamina will start regenerating once it reaches the Base Stamina threshold for that specific Level). Additionally, Moth Jelly restores 35% Sanity upon consumption.
sections.title: Speed Boost

sections.html:
• Moth Jelly is the rarest edible Item in the game, despite Liquid Pain having a rarity of Legendary.
    • This is because Liquid Pain can be found in 5 levels while Moth Jelly can only be found in 2.
  • In the original Backrooms lore, Moth Jelly is produced by Female Deathmoths, instead of Male Deathmoths.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: Gives you a temporary speed and stamina boost

items.name: Rope

sections.html:
• Model
  • Icon
  • Former Icon
sections.title: Rope

sections.html:
File Name

BP_Rope

Icon Name

T_UI_Inv_Icon_Rope

Rope is an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a brown rope.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: Rope is a thick weaved brown rope, similar to ones used for hiking.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • Level 0
  • The Grassrooms
sections.title: Location

sections.html:
Using the Item

Upon using, a climbable rope line comes down, which can be retracted.

Purpose

If you're playing with 2 or more people, Rope lines can be dropped off ledges which other players can interact with to climb up them. This allows to help them get out of pits or to scale up walls. The item is pointless if you're playing alone.
sections.title: Use

sections.html: • This is the earliest on item you can find in the game, since the Flashlight in the liminal house of Level 0 takes a bit longer to get to.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: Pull your teammates out of lower elevations.

items.name: Spray Paint

sections.html:
File Name

N/A

Icon Name

N/A


Spray Paint is an Upcoming Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a spray can.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: Spray Paint resembles Bug Spray but with text on the back and it has the ability to Spray Paint arrows on the wall.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item is planned to be found in:

  • Level 0.1
sections.title: Location

sections.html: From what has been seen of Spray Paint, upon using, a preview of an Arrow will appear on the wall and can be moved anywhere. Upon clicking again, an Arrow will be formed.
sections.title: Use

sections.html: • The reason this Item is being added is to have a way to find your Items if you die.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: N/A

items.name: Thermometer

sections.html:
• Model
  • Icon
  • Former Icon
sections.title: Thermometer

sections.html:
File Name

BP_Thermometer

Icon Name

T_UI_Inv_Icon_Thermometer

Thermometers are an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a temperature measurer tool.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: Thermometers appear as yellow boxes with some black buttons and a screen that reads the temperature of the room you're in using Fahrenheit.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • Level 5 - Terror Hotel
sections.title: Location

sections.html:
Using the Item

The Thermometer cannot be used.

Purpose

Temperature

On the screen of the Thermometer is a temperature reading. It will change depending on what kind of room you're in, which can be useful for detecting Female Deathmoths.

  • 72°F: Nothing is nearby
  • 120°F-133°F: A Female Deathmoth is nearby. A Hive may also be nearby, but not always
  • 140°F: A Hive is nearby
  • 788°F. You are being taken to a Hive by a Female Deathmoth
sections.title: Use

sections.html:
• Along with Almond Concentrate, the Thermometer is one of the only Items in the Game that cannot be used.
  • The Thermometer may be based on a removed object on the Backrooms Fandom Wiki. This Object is Object 71.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: Detects the temperature of the environment

Tickets are an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a red Ticket.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: The Ticket is a old red Ticket with the word "Ticket" and 3 numbers written on it. The numbers are 931.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • Level 3999
sections.title: Location

sections.html:
Using the Item

The Ticket cannot be used.

Purpose

The numbers written on the Ticket are the code to the Elevator in the miniature arcade in Level 3999. Put in the numbers (931) and the Elevator will send you to Level 0.2.
sections.title: Use

items.summary: Contains the code for something...

File Name

Toys are an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a teddy bear.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: The Toy is a small stuffed teddy bear plushie with frequent glowing purple eyes. The purple eyes seem to occur to let the player know where the Toy is at, since a little song also plays whenever one's eyes are purple.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • Level 974
sections.title: Location

sections.html:
Using the Item

Upon using, the player drops the Toy on the ground.

Purpose

In Level 974, you must collect 12 Toys and put them into chairs in the stage room. This is required for escaping Level 974.
sections.title: Use

sections.html: • Although it is a bear, it makes cat sounds when it is picked up and placed on a chair.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: A cute stuffed animal

items.name: Walkie Talkie

sections.title: Walkie Talkie

sections.html:
File Name

N/A

Icon Name

N/A

Walkie Talkies are an Item in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to simply be a Walkie Talkie.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: Walkie Talkies resemble a typical walkie talkie but with the M.E.G. logo on it. Walkie Talkies are made for when playing with 2 or more people.
sections.title: Appearance

sections.html:
This Item can be found in:

  • The Grassrooms
  • Level 8 - Cave System
  • Level 0.11
  • Level 9 - Darkened Suburbs
  • Level 10
  • Level 0.2
  • Level 188
  • Level 55.1
sections.title: Location

sections.html: Upon using, the player will press the button on the Walkie Talkie and an Alarm will play for a few seconds. If you're playing with 2 or more people, you can also use it to communicate with your teammates.
sections.title: Use

sections.html:
• Walkie-Talkies are the most useless item when playing alone, but more or less used with 2 or more people.
  • This is one of the glitchiest items, due to its weird in-game physics when the player comes in contact with it.
    • This strategy is used by most speedrunners to bypass the level without solving the puzzles, specifically in Level 9.
sections.title: Trivia

items.summary: Allows you to communicate with your teammates from a distance.

items.name: Wax Bar

sections.title: Wax Bar

sections.html:
File Name

Wax_bar (Moth Jelly name)

Icon Name

T_UI_Inv_Icon_WaxBar (Moth Jelly name)

The Wax Bar is the name of what was once believed to be an upcoming Item in Escape the Backrooms.
sections.title: Files

sections.html: If you look in the game files of Escape the Backrooms using the UE Viewer, you might find files for Moth Jelly. The file names for Moth Jelly do not call it Moth Jelly, but instead Wax Bar. Because of this, it was once believed to be a new Item, however this was not true. A Wax Bar Item might come to the game in the future, however for now, this does not seem planned. It is also not known why Moth Jelly is called Wax Bar in the game files.
sections.title: Information

sections.html: N/A
sections.title: Location

sections.html: N/A
sections.title: Use

items.summary: File Name Wax_bar (Moth Jelly name) Icon Name T_UI_Inv_Icon_WaxBar (Moth Jelly name) The Wax Bar is the name of what was once believed to be an upcoming Item in Escape the Backrooms.
